rationale: I approved r1 when internal/bead was read-only. The rework now edits internal/bead/bdcli.go (fail-closed MergeMetadata), and the plan's domain-parity fix is mechanically wrong. The complete-time ADR-divergence gate derives its in-scope (candidate) domain set from the SPEC's `## Impacted Domains` via contextpack.ParseSpec -> meta.Domains (divergence.go:104,155,162), and attributeDomainCached (ownership.go:373) restricts attribution to THAT set only. Spec 114's Impacted Domains is workflow-only and explicitly says 'no change lands there' for internal/bead (spec.md:34). So the Bead-2 edit attributes to execution, which is NOT in {workflow} -> the 'not claimed by any OWNERSHIP.yaml for the spec's impacted domains' hard error at divergence.go:221 fires BEFORE the ADR-coverage check (:260). Citing ADR-0037 (which does cover execution) never gets consulted. There is no per-bead 'OWNERSHIP attribution' input to this gate, so the plan's fix does not land. Decomposition/provenance otherwise sound.
concrete_changes_required: Correct the domain-gate mechanism: the complete-time ADR-divergence gate reads its candidate/in-scope domain set from the SPEC's `## Impacted Domains` (contextpack.ParseSpec -> meta.Domains; divergence.go:104/155/162), and attributeDomainCached (ownership.go:373) attributes ONLY within that set. A file whose owner (execution) is absent from the set hits the 'not claimed by any OWNERSHIP.yaml for the spec's impacted domains' error at divergence.go:221 — BEFORE the ADR-coverage check at :260 that the plan relies on. So citing ADR-0037 does NOT prevent the block, and there is no 'Bead 2 brief OWNERSHIP attribution' input to this gate. Remove/replace the claim at plan lines 105-109 and 501-502.

Choose a real lever and specify it: EITHER (a) amend the spec's `## Impacted Domains` (spec.md:34) to add execution / internal/bead (reconcile with ADR-0023 forward-only lifecycle, since the spec is already approved), OR (b) mandate that Bead 2 complete with `--override-adr "<reason>"` and record the reason — the precedented lever (spec 110 used --override-adr for redact.go's core-domain edit). Update the Decomposition domain note, the Bead 2 Scope/Steps, and ADR-0023 fitness accordingly.
Minor: add the Bead-2 already-closed recovery-path audit test (Bead 2 Tests item vi) to the Provenance table row for AC11 — it currently appears only in Verification bullets and Testing Strategy.

findings.note: candidateDomains = spec Impacted Domains (workflow-only); internal/bead edit attributes to execution -> unowned-within-scope hard failure at divergence.go:221. ADR-0037 execution coverage at :260 is unreachable. Verified ownership.go:373 restricts attribution to the passed domain set.

Plan's fix ('Bead 2 brief declares execution in its OWNERSHIP attribution', lines 105-109/501-502) targets a non-existent gate input. Correct levers: edit spec Impacted Domains, or --override-adr at Bead 2 complete (per spec 110 precedent).

3 beads serial A->B->C, longest chain 3 (<=3). Bead 2 larger (internal/bead + write-before-close + dedup) but coherently one durability mechanism; isolation from Bead 1 justified by AC5 diff-review + audit-reorder review surface. One-round verifiable once the domain-gate lever is fixed.

TOCTOU + post-write erasure + fail-closed MergeMetadata + dedup all mapped (AC11/AC12 rows). 3 added AC5 fixtures (f/g/h) mapped in AC5 row. Gap: already-closed recovery-path test (Bead 2 tests vi) missing from Provenance table.

Verified: bdcli.go MergeMetadata proceeds-from-empty on read err (~263-290) as plan describes; internal/bead/** owned by execution; ADR-0037 Domain(s)=workflow,execution; completeMergeMetadataFn seam and candidateDomains derivation all check out.
